title: hieracium murorum subsp. tholerotrichum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. tholerotrichum, also known as Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), characterized by its yellow or orange flowers and a rosette of leaves. The medicinal properties of Hawkweed lie in its ability to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and fungal infections, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies. The health benefits of Hawkweed include its potential to treat eczema, acne, and other skin conditions, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce fever. Hawkweed can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote wound healing and soothe sore muscles.
